question: [File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: liquidate / IERC20.transfer / bitmap] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under the precondition that the collateral token's transfer to receiver fails silently (returns true, transfers 0), trigger the sequence liquidate(seizedAssets>0) -> collateral[i] reduced, bit cleared if newCollateral==0 -> safeTransfer(token, receiver, seizedAssets) transfers 0, violating the invariant that seized collateral must be transferred to the liquidator, causing scoped impact: collateral[i] reduced and bit cleared but liquidator receives nothing, effectively burning borrower's collateral without compensating liquidator, discouraging liquidations? Proof idea: deploy no-op transfer ERC20; assert liquidator receives seizedAssets tokens.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: isHealthy / liquidate / bitmap with LLTV=1] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under the precondition that a market has LLTV=1 (WAD) for a collateral and the borrower has only that collateral active, trigger the sequence isHealthy -> maxDebt = collateral[i].mulDivDown(price, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivDown(WAD, WAD) = collateral[i]*price/ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE -> any debt <= maxDebt is healthy, violating the invariant that LLTV=1 positions with bad debt can always be fully liquidated (RCF deactivated), causing scoped impact: verify that the bitmap loop correctly computes maxDebt for LLTV=1 and that the RCF formula (lltv < WAD check) correctly deactivates RCF? Proof idea: fuzz LLTV=1 positions; assert maxRepaid = type(uint256).max and full liquidation is always allowed.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: supplyCollateral / bitmap / token with 0 decimals] Can an unprivileged borrower, under the precondition that the collateral token has 0 decimals and the oracle returns a price scaled to ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE (1e36), trigger the sequence supplyCollateral(assets=1) -> collateral[i]=1 -> isHealthy: maxDebt = 1.mulDivDown(price, 1e36).mulDivDown(lltv, WAD) -> if price < 1e36, maxDebt = 0 -> position immediately unhealthy, violating the invariant that supplying collateral must improve or maintain health, causing scoped impact: borrower supplies collateral but health check still fails due to price/decimal mismatch, bitmap bit set but collateral contributes 0 to maxDebt, enabling liquidation of a position that just received collateral? Proof idea: fuzz token decimals (0, 6, 8, 18, 27) and oracle price; assert maxDebt > 0 when collateral > 0 and price > 0.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: liquidate / bitmap / collateralIndex not in bitmap with repaidUnits>0] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under the precondition that collateralIndex is not in the borrower's collateralBitmap (bit not set) but repaidUnits > 0, trigger the sequence liquidate(collateralIndex=i_not_in_bitmap, repaidUnits>0) -> bad debt loop does not set liquidatedCollatPrice (i never matches) -> liquidatedCollatPrice=0 -> seizedAssets = repaidUnits.mulDivDown(lif, WAD).mulDivDown(ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE, 0) -> division by zero revert, violating the invariant that liquidate should revert with a meaningful error rather than a division-by-zero panic, causing scoped impact: liquidator's transaction reverts unexpectedly; verify this is the correct behavior and not exploitable? Proof idea: call liquidate with collateralIndex not in bitmap and repaidUnits>0; assert revert with division-by-zero.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: supplyCollateral / bitmap / MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER boundary] Can an unprivileged borrower, under the precondition that they have exactly 16 active collaterals (MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER), trigger the sequence supplyCollateral(collateralIndex=j, assets=0) where collateral[j]==0 -> oldCollateral=0, assets=0, condition oldCollateral==0 && assets>0 is false, bit NOT set, countBits check NOT triggered, violating the invariant that supplying 0 assets to a new collateral index does not corrupt the bitmap, causing scoped impact: no-op supply with assets=0 is silently accepted, bitmap unchanged, but verify this cannot be used to bypass the 16-collateral limit by pre-staging a zero-asset supply? Proof idea: fuzz assets=0 with 16 active collaterals; assert countBits(bitmap) <= 16 and no bit is set for zero-asset supply.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: liquidate / isHealthy / bitmap loop termination] Can an unprivileged attacker, under the precondition that a borrower's collateralBitmap is type(uint128).max (all 128 bits set) and the market has only 1 collateral in collateralParams, trigger the sequence isHealthy -> bitmap loop: msb returns 127 -> market.collateralParams[127] -> array out-of-bounds revert, violating the invariant that the bitmap cannot have bits set beyond the market's collateral count, causing scoped impact: permanent DoS on all health-check-dependent operations for the borrower? Proof idea: verify supplyCollateral prevents setting bits beyond collateralParams.length; assert collateralBitmap bits are always < market.collateralParams.length.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: liquidate / bitmap / seizedAssets underflow] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under the precondition that seizedAssets > collateral[collateralIndex] (liquidator requests more than available), trigger the sequence liquidate(seizedAssets > collateral[i]) -> newCollateral = collateral[i] - seizedAssets -> Solidity 0.8 underflow revert, violating the invariant that liquidate should revert with a meaningful error, causing scoped impact: liquidation reverts; verify this is not exploitable to DoS the liquidation path or that the bitmap is not partially modified before the revert? Proof idea: fuzz seizedAssets > collateral[i]; assert revert occurs before any state change; verify bitmap is unchanged on revert.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: supplyCollateral / withdrawCollateral / bitmap / authorized operator] Can an unprivileged authorized operator, under the precondition that the borrower has debt > 0 and the operator calls withdrawCollateral(collateralIndex=i, assets=collateral[i]) reducing collateral[i] to 0 and clearing bit i, trigger the sequence withdrawCollateral -> isHealthy called -> bitmap no longer includes collateral i -> if remaining collateral is insufficient, revert with UnhealthyBorrower, violating the invariant that an authorized operator cannot make a healthy position unhealthy, causing scoped impact: operator correctly blocked; but verify that the health check uses the post-withdrawal bitmap and not the pre-withdrawal bitmap? Proof idea: assert isHealthy is called after bitmap update in withdrawCollateral.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: liquidate / bitmap / post-maturity mode] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under the precondition that the market has passed maturity and the borrower has multiple active collaterals, trigger the sequence liquidate(postMaturityMode=true, collateralIndex=i) -> bad debt loop runs over all bitmap bits -> lif ramps from 1 to maxLif over 15 minutes -> seize section uses lif for collateralIndex=i only, violating the invariant that post-maturity liquidation correctly accounts for all active collaterals in bad debt calculation while using the ramped lif only for the seized collateral, causing scoped impact: verify bad debt is not over-realized due to bitmap including collaterals not yet fully valued at ramped lif? Proof idea: fuzz timestamp between maturity and maturity+15min; assert badDebt computation is consistent with actual collateral values.
